name: per-source extends: include.operator_namespaces:[acme] activates only _operator/acme/ rules (not other operator namespaces)
command:
mkdir -p tree/_operator/acme tree/_operator/contoso && for ns in acme contoso; do printf "source:
  id: %s-src
  authoritative_publisher: %s
  authoritative_url: https://%s.com
  registry_link: STANDARDS-URLS.yaml
  fetched_at: 2026-05-13T00:00:00Z
  content_hash: sha256:S
  fetch_frequency: daily
  fragility_tier: low
  license_note: MIT
rules:
  - {id: %s-rule, name: x, description: x, detector: x, type: problem, fixable: null, has_suggestions: false, deprecated: false, replaced_by: [], docs_url: https://x.com, requires_type_checking: false, recommended: true, options_schema: {}, messages: {default: x}, severity: P1, version: 1.0.0, semver: 1.0.0, rule_state: warn-only, provenance: [{source: x, class: published-standard, section_id: x, url: https://x.com, fetched_at: 2026-05-13T00:00:00Z, content_hash: sha256:S}]}
recommended_set: [%s-rule]
all_set: [%s-rule]
" "$ns" "$ns" "$ns" "$ns" "$ns" "$ns" > "tree/_operator/$ns/x.yaml"; done && printf "include:
  operator_namespaces: [acme]
" > profile.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/profiles/active.sh" profile.yaml --tree "$PWD/tree" --emit-resolved 2>out.json && grep "acme-rule" out.json 1>&2 && ! grep "contoso-rule" out.json && echo ok 1>&2
